questions.question: Choose the option that is unrelated (or odd one out amongst) the given set of words.
questions.explanation: All the words except 'schizophrenia' can be used to describe various human personality types. Schizophrenia is a mental disorder.

questions.question: In the question, the word is represented by only one set of the number as given in any one of the alternatives. The sets of numbers given in the alternatives are represented by two classes of alphabets as in two matrices given below. The columns and rows of Matrix 1 are numbered from 0 to 4 and columns and rows of matrix 2 are numbered from 5 to 9. A letter from this matrix can be represented first by its row and then by its column. Ex. A can be represented as 98, 75 and B can be represented as 24, 41, 57 etc. Similarly, you have to identify the set for the word "PINE". ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3696/image009.png)
questions.explanation: P = 68 I = 30/23/59 N = 34 E = 02/13 So, option 2 is the correct answer.

questions.question: Six friends P, Q, R, S, T and U were aged differently. Number of persons elder to Q was thrice as that the number of persons younger to S. P was younger to S. U was younger to R but elder to T. Who was the 2ⁿᵈ eldest persons?
questions.explanation: • Number of persons elder to Q was thrice as that the number of persons younger to S. • P was younger to S. __ > __ > __ > Q > S > P • U was younger to R but elder to T R > U > T > Q > S > P Thus, U was the 2ⁿᵈ eldest person.

questions.question: In the following question, select the odd letters from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: All except option (c) follows the same logic. →First alphabet + 5 = third alphabet and second alphabet + 5 = fourth alphabet H+5=M; Y+5=D J+5=O; W+5=B F+5=K; S+5=X

questions.question: In the following question, select the odd number pair from the given alternatives.

Identify the diagram that best represents the relationship among the given classes. Widows, women, doctors (a) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3821/image054.png) (b) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3821/image055.png) (c) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3821/image056.png) (d) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3821/image057.png)
questions.explanation: All widows are women. Some women as well as some widows are doctors. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3821/image058.png)

questions.question: If 4 / 3 + 1 = 48 and 2 / 5 + 7 = 280, then in the expression M / 3 + 5 = 150, what is the value of 'M'?
questions.explanation: The logic followed here is: Step I: M*3*5 = 15M Step II: 15M*4= 150 M = 150/60 = 2.5 M=2.5

questions.question: In the following question, select the related number from the given alternatives. 287 : 104 ∷ 379 : ?

In each of the questions below are given some statements followed by some conclusions. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts. Read all the conclusions and then decide which of the given conclusions logically follows from the given statements disregarding commonly known facts. Statements: Some dams are pens. Some pens are rivers. All rivers are cars. Conclusions: I. Some cars are pens. II. Some cars are dams
options: If either Conclusion I or II follows.
If neither Conclusion I nor II follows.
questions.explanation: The statements can be represented as: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/syllogism/image0275893_dams_pens.jpg) Thus only conclusion I follows

questions.question: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/sscimg/6700/image016.png) Which number will come opposite to number 2 ?
questions.explanation: From (i) and (ii) we can see that the numbers 1, 4, 5 and 6 appear adjacent to 2. Hence 3 appears opposite to 2.

questions.question: BATON: MUSIC CONDUCTOR :: ? : JUDGE
questions.explanation: Baton is a type of stick used by a music conductor. Similarly a court judge uses a short hammer which is also called a Gavel.

questions.question: If " HEADSTART " is coded as "KRDQSRMPF", then what is the code of "ANTIVIRUS"?
questions.explanation: The word HEADSTART is divided into two groups of 4 words each and the middle letter remains unchanged. H E A D S T A R T (+3) (+13) (+3) (+13) (-2) (-14) (-2) (-14) K R D Q S R M P F Similarly, A N T I V I R U S (+3) (+13) (+3) (+13) (-2) (-14) (-2) (-14) D A W V V G D S E

questions.question: In each of the following questions, choose the correct mirror image of the figure, from amongst the four alternatives. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/sscimg/6031/image010.png)
questions.explanation: In a mirror image, the figure is flipped along the vertical plane. So the line with the dot on top right will now have it on the top left. Also the horizontal line emerging out on the left will now be to the right.

questions.question: Find the missing term. 126, 68, 36, ___, 26
Arrange the following words as per the order in dictionary. 1. Expiry 2. Exigency 3. Experiment 4. Expensive 5. Experienced
questions.explanation: This is the correct form as per the order in dictionary. Exigency> Expensive> Experienced> Experiment> Expiry

questions.question: A piece of paper is folded and cut as shown below in the question figures, from the given answer figures, indicate how it will appear when opened. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/other/s15054.png) A. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p904/image025.png) B. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p904/image026.png) C. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p904/image027.png) D. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p904/image028.png)
questions.explanation: The option 1 correct.

questions.question: From the given alternative words, select the one which can be formed using the letters of the given word. MAGISTRATE
questions.explanation: Only the word SEAT can be formed by using the given word.

questions.question: Find the missing number in the given matrix. 12241224 205010100 8204?
questions.explanation: In every row, the third number is the HCF of the first two numbers while the fourth number is their LCM. Hence the missing number is LCM of 8 and 20, i.e. 40.

questions.question: Select the related word/letters/number from the given alternatives. FHBJ : LPDT :: EKCG : ?
questions.explanation: For this FHBJ : LPDT The respective numbers of the letter were multiplied by 2. i.e., F => 6, 6 x 2 = 12 => L. H => 8, 8 x 2 = 16 => P. B => 2, 2 x 2 = 4 => D. J => 10, 10 x 2 = 20 => T. Similarly follow the above logic EKCG becomes JVFN.

questions.question: Count the number of triangle in the given figure? ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1848/image034.png)
questions.explanation: We first label the figure: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1848/image035.png) Now the triangle can be easily counted, they are APH, AIP, DGT, MDT, NUV, NQR, NRY, VRW, LSB, BSE, ZXC, FXC, JRN, JRQ, KRJ

questions.question: 3² x 2² ÷ (36 - 9) + 1² = 33; two signs in the equations given have been interchanged. Find out these two signs to make the equation correct.
questions.explanation: Interchanging symbols - and ÷ as given in option (D), the given equation becomes 9 x 4 - (36 ÷ 9) + 1 = 36 - 4 + 1 = 33

questions.question: P has to move toward south in order to reach N and towards east in order to reach R, who is to the north of Q. P and R have to travel in north-east and north-west directions respectively in order to reach L. In which direction L must travel in order to reach Q?
questions.explanation: L must travel South-east in order to reach Q. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/96444/image001.jpg)

questions.question: Find the missing terms. ABCD, CCFF, FDIH, JELJ, OFOL, ?
questions.explanation: The first letter increases by 2, 3, 4, 5, 6; the second letter by 1; the third letter by 3 and the fourth letter by 2. Hence the next term is UGRN.

questions.question: The question figure is embedded and hidden in which of the options figure? ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1851/image043.png) A. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1851/image044.png) B. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1851/image045.png) C. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1851/image046.png) D. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1851/image047.png)
questions.explanation: In option 1, both the triangles opposite to each other are not symmetrical as shown in the question figure or you can observe the diagonal is misplaced. Option 2 does not have 2 diagonals as that of in question figure. In option 4, there is no vertical line passing from the dividing point of two diagonals. Option 3 is the correct answer as it is embedded in the question figure. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1851/image048.png)
